# GitHub Copilot Instructions for cargo-dist

## Project Overview

cargo-dist (binary: `dist`) is a tool for shippable application packaging. It builds binaries, generates installers (shell, PowerShell, Homebrew, npm, MSI, macOS .pkg), and creates CI pipelines (GitHub Actions) for automated releases of Rust and other projects.

## Workspace Layout

This is a Cargo workspace with three crates:
- `cargo-dist/` — Main CLI and library. Contains all core logic.
- `cargo-dist-schema/` — JSON schema for `dist-manifest.json`.
- `axoproject/` — Project/workspace discovery (Cargo, npm, generic).

## Language and Toolchain

- Rust edition 2021, MSRV 1.74, toolchain 1.93
- First-party dependencies: axoasset, axocli, axoprocess, axotag, axoupdater

## Code Conventions

- `#![deny(missing_docs)]` is enforced — add doc comments to all public items
- Use `camino::Utf8Path` / `Utf8PathBuf` instead of `std::path::Path`
- Use `miette` for error reporting and `thiserror` for error type definitions
- Return `DistResult<T>` from fallible functions
- Use `tracing` macros for logging, not `println!` or `log`
- Use `axoprocess::Cmd` for running subprocesses, not `std::process::Command`
- Use `axoasset::LocalAsset` for file I/O

## Architecture Quick Reference

- `tasks.rs` — Central planner; `gather_work()` builds the `DistGraph`
- `config/` — Config parsing (v0 in Cargo.toml metadata, v1 in dist-workspace.toml)
- `build/` — Build execution (cargo, generic, fake/mock)
- `backend/ci/` — GitHub Actions CI generation
- `backend/installer/` — Shell, PowerShell, Homebrew, npm, MSI, macOS .pkg generators
- `backend/templates.rs` — Minijinja templates (source: `cargo-dist/templates/`)
- `announce.rs` — Release notes and changelogs
- `sign/` — Code signing (macOS, SSL.com)
- `manifest.rs` — dist-manifest.json output

## Testing

- Snapshot tests with `insta`. Run `cargo insta review` after `cargo test`.
- Tests must run serially (no nextest or parallel runners).
- Mock builds in `build::fake` module.

## Commands

- `cargo build` — Build
- `cargo test` — Test (then `cargo insta review`)
- `cargo clippy --workspace` — Lint
- `cargo fmt --all` — Format
- `typos` — Spell check
